text: Delivered gale-parity module (parity/, JVM-only, Breeze 2.1.0 test-scope — native Scala 3 artifact, no for3Use2_13 needed; core/laws/bench classpaths stay Breeze-free, testAll unchanged, new parityTest alias). 25 tests / 4 suites, all green: DenseOps (8: products, transpose products, dot — 1e-12 elementwise on bit-identical logical inputs), Factorization (8: det incl. sign under row swap, solve, LU-reused multi-RHS solve, LU/Cholesky/QR invariants, RtR=AtA sign-free cross-parity, NEW least-squares vs breeze backslash on tall systems), Spectral (5: eigSym vs eigSymmetric ascending eigenvalues, sign-aware + subspace-aware eigenvector comparison, iterative Lanczos top-k vs eigSym extremes), BandedSparse (4). Fresh-context adversarial review focused on VACUITY: verdict clean — no vacuous tests, no cancelling layout errors (both sides fill via logical (i,j) tabulate), all failures throw, deterministic seeds incl. the LCG Lanczos start vector; Breeze APIs verified used correctly. Review follow-ups applied by lead: least-squares comparison added (the one real coverage gap — QR path as a solver vs independent reference), det/recon tolerances tightened 1e-9 -> 1e-11 (still green). SVD + nonsymmetric eig parity correctly out of scope (private[gale], no public entry points yet) — extend when phase-a partial SVD and phase-b land. GOAL EVIDENCE: gale matches Breeze across the shared public surface at 1e-10..1e-12.
